close_reason: Calibration note, not an issue to fix
description:
Calibration of the rusty pass on secretx-cache.

Probability a P0/P1 bug was missed: LOW (~5%).

Rationale:
- Crate is 240 lines lib + 204 lines tests; small enough to read end-to-end multiple times.
- All concurrency surfaces are explicit: one Mutex, one Arc, two impl blocks, three async methods. No hidden invariants.
- Lock discipline is documented and verified by reading: get(), refresh(), put() each release the lock before any await on inner.
- Cache contents are bounded: a single Option<CachedEntry>. No collection growth, no eviction policy bugs possible.
- TTL math uses Instant::elapsed() and Duration comparison, both well-defined under monotonic-Instant assumption (correct on all tier-1 targets).
- No unsafe code.
- Generic bound issue (CachingStore<dyn SecretStore>) was caught and filed.
- Zeroize roundtrip issue was caught and filed.
- Pre-flight (clippy -D warnings, hack feature-powerset, nightly docsrs doc) all clean.

Areas where a bug COULD hide that I did not exhaustively explore:
- Interaction with secretx-core's WritableSecretStore put-then-get atomicity guarantee under concurrent put/get from multiple CachingStore instances pointing at the same inner Arc. Beyond the scope of this single-crate review.
- Behavior under tokio runtime shutdown while a lock is held. Standard Rust drop semantics apply; no obvious bug.

Findings filed: 8 (1 P1 idiom, 2 P2 design, 3 P3 cleanup, 2 P4 opinion). No P0.

Convergence signal: a follow-up rusty pass on this crate after these are addressed should produce only P3/P4 opinion-tagged findings or none.
title: Section 7: confidence calibration for secretx-cache rusty review
